topic: AI agents in the enterprise — the 2026 inflection from demo to deployed
main_argument: 2026 is the year enterprise AI agents cross from pilots into production budgets, and the durable value accrues not to the foundation-model labs but to the orchestration, evaluation and integration layer that makes agents reliable enough to trust with real workflows.

audience.who: VC and growth-stage investors evaluating where to deploy capital in the AI-agent stack
audience.what_they_know: fluent in SaaS metrics and the model landscape (Anthropic, OpenAI); skeptical of demo-driven hype; have heard the 'agents replace SaaS seats' thesis and want it pressure-tested
audience.what_they_want: a defensible read on where margin and moat actually sit in the stack, what is real revenue vs. vaporware, and which layer to underwrite

length_format: long-form analytical article, ~2,000-2,500 words, Markdown, inline numbered citations + references list, Substack/industry-blog ready

style.tone: confident, analytical, mildly contrarian — opinions welcome, every number sourced
style.person: first person singular with direct second-person address to the investor
style.pov: the value migrates up the stack to orchestration/eval/integration; foundation models commoditize into infrastructure

call_to_action: underwrite the orchestration-and-reliability layer, not another foundation-model bet or a thin GPT wrapper; ask portfolio companies for agent task-completion and rollback metrics, not demo videos
source_questions_resolved: existing sources (none yet — research branch supplies them)
source: content-research-writer step 1 — Understand the Writing Project (six clarifying questions resolved into explicit decisions)
